steps.title: What meaning does the blank need?
steps.content: Tallying patents ______ the degree of innovation because truly novel patents are vastly outnumbered by nonnovel ones. A raw count makes innovation look different from what it really is — the blank must mean misrepresents or gives a false picture of.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The [word] needs to mean gives a false impression of. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — belies: gives a false impression of, contradicts — a count dominated by nonnovel patents belies (misrepresents) the true level of innovation. ✓
  • Choice B — portends: foretells, signals a future event — a patent count is not predicting anything about innovation; it is distorting the current picture. ✗
  • Choice C — thwarts: prevents or blocks — a patent count doesn't stop innovation from occurring; it just misrepresents it. ✗
  • Choice D — maligns: speaks badly of — a count doesn't criticize innovation; it distorts understanding of it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
